MOONLIGHT
BAZAAR IX

For one night, Grannan Street trades sidewalks for orbit. A seven-metre moon rises over the rooftops, the alleys fill with music and firelight, and the whole city looks up.

07.18.2026Saturday · 7PM till late
Free · 19+Open air street event
For All HumanityThis year's theme
Mission Brief

One street. One night. One enormous moon.

Moonlight Bazaar is Saint John's free, open-air night market — a mashup of live music, international food, art installations, and street performance that takes over the historic uptown core for a single evening every summer.

Its signature is impossible to miss: a seven-metre inflated moon, suspended between the rooftops of Grannan Street, glowing over the crowd below. This year's edition, Moonlight Bazaar IX, carries the theme "For All Humanity" — a nod to exploration, wonder, and the idea that a night like this belongs to everyone in the city.

Now in its ninth year, the Bazaar has grown into one of Atlantic Canada's largest street-culture events, drawing thousands into the alleys and squares of uptown Saint John for one unrepeatable night under the moon.

On The Street This Year

Things to find in orbit

Centerpiece

The Moon

The seven-metre illuminated moon returns as the visual anchor of the whole night, suspended over the street from rooftop to rooftop.

Music

Live Music, Multiple Stages

Local and touring acts play across the footprint of the Bazaar, from street-corner sets to the main stage.

Food

International Food & Oyster Bar

A rotating lineup of food vendors and a dedicated oyster bar cover everything from street snacks to a proper sit-down bite.

Market

50+ Local Vendors

Makers, artists, and small businesses from across the region set up shop along the length of the street.

Performance

Fire Throwing & Belly Dancing

Roaming performers keep the crowd's attention split between the sky and the street with fire, dance, and theatrical vignettes.

Mystic

Fortune Tellers

Palm readers, tarot, and other mystical practitioners set up along the route for anyone curious what's written in the stars.

Science

Telescopes with the Astronomy Club

Local astronomers set up telescopes and run space trivia and giveaways for anyone who wants to look past the inflatable moon at the real thing.

Family

Kids Hour, 4–6PM

Before the night crowd arrives, younger visitors come out in costume for a dedicated hour of prizes and family-friendly activities.

See you under the moon.
